agency: New York City Office of the City Clerk (OCC)
method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, robots-respecting). Fingerprinted the informational site cityclerk.nyc.gov (AWS ALB + Akamai + nginx + Dynatrace, jQuery/Bootstrap, WebTrends) which is now driven by the NYC.gov Content API (apps.nyc.gov/content-api/v1/content + /v2/nav — a real, undocumented JSON backend). Probed Project Cupid (projectcupid.cityofnewyork.us/app/cupidceremony), the online marriage-license/appointment system, identified as a no-code application on the rented Unqork platform (polyfill.unqork.io, /fbu/ uapi, Angular SPA). Probed e-Lobbyist (apps.nyc.gov/elobbyist), a login-walled Java application (JSESSIONID, .do) behind SAP CDC / Gigya SSO, and the public Lobbyist Search (lobbyistsearch.nyc.gov, JS SPA over a .do Java backend). Verified the NYC Open Data agency label 'Office of the City Clerk (OCC)' via the Socrata Discovery API and pulled all 2 assets with column schemas.
headline_findings: The City Clerk runs two bureaus on rented, agent-invisible platforms: the Marriage Bureau's Project Cupid is a no-code application on the rented Unqork platform, and the Lobbying Bureau's e-Lobbyist is a login-walled Java app behind rented SAP CDC / Gigya SSO.

The flagship citizen transaction — applying for a marriage license — has NO API and NO Open Data twin at all. It lives only inside Project Cupid's Unqork screens; NYC's most personal civic transaction is outsourced to a no-code vendor.

The Lobbying Bureau is the more open half: registration and periodic filing happen in e-Lobbyist, but the reported data IS published — two NYC Open Data datasets (City Clerk eLobbyist Data fmf3-knd8, 28c; Lobbyists' Fundraising & Political Consulting Reports 7arw-dbem, 24c) plus a public Lobbyist Search.
Marriage records are NOT the City Clerk's on Open Data — only DORIS publishes historical vital-records indexes (j62e-7maa, d8dr-nyhw). The live marriage side is entirely closed.

The informational site was quietly modernized onto the NYC.gov Content API (apps.nyc.gov/content-api/v1) — a real JSON backend serving content and navigation — but it is undocumented, shared, and read-only.
Net-new write surface: apply for a marriage license (MarriageLicenseApplication) — the single most common Marriage Bureau transaction, with no API today. See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md.

apis_observed.note: Real JSON backend that drives cityclerk.nyc.gov (content + navigation). Read-only, undocumented, shared across NYC.gov agency sites. Content only — no transactions.

Online marriage-license application, appointment scheduling, and ceremony booking. Built on the rented Unqork no-code platform (polyfill.unqork.io, /fbu/ uapi, Angular SPA). No documented API, no OpenAPI, no Open Data output. The net-new write surface (MarriageLicenseApplication).
e-Lobbyist registration and periodic filing. Java servlet app (JSESSIONID, .do) fronted by SAP CDC / Gigya SAML SSO (fidm.us1.gigya.com). No public API; reported data is published to Open Data.
Lobbyist Search public database — JS SPA (webpack bundle) over a .do Java backend. Public but no documented/OpenAPI contract.
2 City Clerk datasets (lobbyist filing + fundraising reports), each a SODA /resource/<id>.json endpoint. The only open, machine-readable City Clerk API — and only for the lobbying side.
City Clerk Forms Online (officiant registration, records requests). Server-rendered Java forms app; no API.

fruit.name: Apply for a marriage license
fruit.notes: The single most common Marriage Bureau transaction — apply for a NYC marriage license. No API and no Open Data twin; lives only inside the Unqork-hosted Project Cupid system. The net-new WRITE surface for this domain.

fruit.name: Book a marriage / domestic-partnership ceremony (appointment)
fruit.notes: Schedule a civil marriage or domestic-partnership ceremony / license appointment. Availability and booking are Project Cupid (Unqork) only; no API, no Open Data. An alternative net-new write/read surface (find_appointments).

fruit.name: Marriage license (issued record)
fruit.notes: The issued marriage-license record. No public API and no Open Data twin; only DORIS publishes historical vital-records indexes (j62e-7maa, d8dr-nyhw), not the live City Clerk record.

fruit.name: Marriage officiant registration
fruit.notes: Register as a marriage officiant / one-day officiant. Java forms application (City Clerk Forms Online); no API, no Open Data.

fruit.name: Lobbyist registration / Lobbyist Search
fruit.notes: Registered lobbyists and their clients. Filing is login-walled e-Lobbyist (Java + Gigya SSO), but the registrant/client identity is published via the eLobbyist Data dataset and the public Lobbyist Search.

fruit.name: Lobbyist periodic filings (compensation, expenses, activities)
fruit.notes: Periodic lobbying reports — compensation, expense, activity, and target detail per period. The City Clerk's most-viewed dataset. Filing itself is e-Lobbyist-only; the reported data is open.

fruit.name: Lobbyist fundraising & political-consulting reports
opendata_match: Lobbyists' Fundraising and Political Consulting Reports 7arw-dbem (24c)
fruit.notes: Fundraising and political-consulting activity by lobbyists on behalf of candidates. Open Data twin exists; filed via e-Lobbyist.

fruit.name: Commissioner of Deeds / Oath of Office
fruit.notes: Additional Services: become a Commissioner of Deeds, file an Oath of Office, obtain City Council transcripts. Forms/PDF only; no API, no Open Data. A registry-style transaction with no machine-readable surface.

meta.platform: cityclerk.nyc.gov on the NYC.gov Content API (AWS ALB + Akamai + nginx + Dynatrace, jQuery/Bootstrap, WebTrends); Project Cupid marriage system on the rented Unqork no-code platform (Angular SPA); e-Lobbyist Java filing app behind SAP CDC / Gigya SSO; Lobbyist Search JS SPA over a .do Java backend
meta.netnew: MarriageLicenseApplication — a couple applies for a NYC marriage license (today only via Project Cupid's Unqork screens)
meta.tagline: The City Clerk rents the machinery of the city's two most personal civic transactions — getting married and registering to lobby — and owns no API contract over either.
meta.headline: The Marriage Bureau's flagship transaction runs on a no-code Unqork app with no API and no data, while the Lobbying Bureau at least publishes its filings — so the modernization move is to put an owned contract in front of both rented platforms.

scorecard_notes.platform: Own domain and a modern content-API-driven site, but the two core transaction systems are rented — Unqork (no-code) for marriage and SAP CDC / Gigya for lobbyist auth.
scorecard_notes.siteApi: A real JSON Content API (apps.nyc.gov/content-api/v1) drives the site, and Lobbyist Search runs on a JSON backend — but both are undocumented, shared, and contract-less.
scorecard_notes.openData: Only 2 datasets, both lobbying; the entire marriage side has no Open Data at all (DORIS publishes only historical vital-records indexes).
scorecard_notes.coreData: Lobbyist registration, filing, and fundraising are richly published, but the bigger half of the domain — marriage licenses, ceremonies — publishes nothing.
scorecard_notes.writeApi: No write API; applying for a marriage license and filing lobbyist reports are both locked in rented apps (Unqork / Java+Gigya).
scorecard_notes.agent: No MCP, no agent-native surface, no documented API for an agent to call.
scorecard_notes.ownership: The flagship citizen transaction (Project Cupid) is a rented no-code app and auth is rented (Gigya); the City Clerk owns its domain and Java apps but not the machinery of its most important transaction.
